title: Video Recording via Playwright recordVideo
overview: Add video start/stop/status commands to browse CLI using Playwright's recordVideo context option. Zero CDP overhead, WebM output, no external deps. Uses existing context recreation pattern.
scopeChallenge: Feature builds entirely on existing recreateContext() and HAR start/stop patterns. No new modules or deps needed. Context recreation with full state preservation is already battle-tested by emulate and useragent commands.

failureModes.risk: page.video() returns null if page was created before recordVideo context
failureModes.mitigation: recreateContext already creates new pages — video() will be set on the new pages

failureModes.risk: Video file not flushed until page/context close
failureModes.mitigation: On stop, we recreate context (which closes old pages) — Playwright flushes video on page close

failureModes.risk: Emulate/useragent while recording drops recordVideo option
failureModes.mitigation: Pass recordVideo through in recreateContext when videoRecording is active
Use fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) before starting

failureModes.risk: WebM file not fully written at assertion time
failureModes.mitigation: video.saveAs() waits for video to be finalized before resolving — no race condition

testCoverageMap.codepath: stopVideoRecording() — video path collection + context recreation

tasks.title: Add video recording state and methods to BrowserManager
tasks.description: Add videoRecording state field and startVideoRecording()/stopVideoRecording()/getVideoRecording() methods to BrowserManager. Start calls recreateContext with recordVideo option. Stop collects page.video() refs, recreateContext without recordVideo (finalizes videos), then video.saveAs(dir/tab-N.webm) for each — works local and remote CDP. Ensure emulateDevice/useragent pass recordVideo through when active.
acceptanceCriteria: startVideoRecording(dir) creates a new context with recordVideo option and preserves all tabs/cookies/state
stopVideoRecording() saves videos via video.saveAs() with predictable names (tab-N.webm) and recreates context without recordVideo
Calling startVideoRecording while already recording throws error
emulateDevice while recording passes recordVideo through to new context

tasks.title: Add video command handler in meta.ts and register in server
tasks.description: Add case 'video' to handleMetaCommand with start/stop/status subcommands following HAR pattern. Register 'video' in META_COMMANDS in server.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: browse video start begins recording, returns confirmation with output directory
browse video stop returns paths of saved WebM files with duration
browse video stop without active recording throws clear error
browse video status returns recording state

tasks.title: Register video command in CLI help and verify chain support
tasks.description: Update CLI help text in src/cli.ts to add video commands to the Recording line. Verify chain command routes video to meta handler.
acceptanceCriteria: browse --help shows video commands in the Recording line
chain command works with video start/stop sequence
video start is NOT in SAFE_TO_RETRY

tasks.title: Integration tests for video recording
tasks.description: Add tests in test/features.test.ts: happy path start/navigate/stop, status checks, double-start error, stop-without-start error, screenshots during recording, emulate during recording. Use temp dir for output.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 test cases pass with real Chromium
WebM files written to disk with non-zero size
Temp video files cleaned up in afterAll

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, and exports-reference docs
tasks.description: Update command counts (total: 75→76, Meta: 24→25, fix stale '23 meta commands' at CLAUDE.md:49 and exports-reference.md:25,147) and add video to command lists in CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/browse/SKILL.md, and .claude/claude-md-refs/exports-reference.md. Check all count references in all three files.
acceptanceCriteria: Total command count is 76 and Meta command count is 25 in CLAUDE.md
video start|stop|status appears in all three docs
No stale command counts remain (check total, meta, and per-category counts in all files)
